title: Building trust in the AI era with privacy-led UX
description: The practice of privacy-led user experience (UX) is a design philosophy that treats transparency around data collection and usage as an integral part of the customer relationship. title: Anthropic’s rise is giving some OpenAI investors second thoughts
description: One investor who has backed both companies told the FT that justifying OpenAI's recent round required assuming an IPO valuation of $1.2 trillion or more — making Anthropic's current $380 billion valuation look like the relative bargain.

title: Ukraine’s military robot surge aims to offset drone risks to humans
description: Ukraine is replacing more soldiers with robots in the battlefield kill zone.

title: Sony killing features for antenna, set-top box users of Bravia smart TVs in May
description: Some 2023 and 2024 models are also affected.

title: Americans ask AI for health care. Hospitals think the answer is more chatbots.
description: Do you trust AI chatbots for health advice? What about one in your patient portal?

title: Two-year-old Surface PCs get $300 price hikes as sub-$1,000 models go away
description: "Paying more for the same stuff" is the story of consumer technology in 2026.

title: Apple chooses Amazon satellites for iPhone, years after rejecting Starlink offer
description: Amazon announces $11.6B merger with Globalstar and satellite deal with Apple.

title: Anthropic co-founder confirms the company briefed the Trump administration on Mythos
description: In an interview at the Semafor World Economy summit this week, Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark explained why the company was still engaged with the U.S. government while simultaneously suing them.

title: Redefining the future of software engineering
description: Software engineering has experienced two seismic shifts this century. First was the rise of the open source movement, which gradually made code accessible to developers and engineers everywhere. title: Max Hodak’s Science Corp. is preparing to place its first sensor in a human brain
description: The device could help address multiple neurological conditions if it proves successful. One early use could be delivering gentle electrical stimulation to damaged brain or spinal cord cells to encourage healing.

title: Google adds AI Skills to Chrome to help you save favorite workflows
description: Google is adding “Skills” to Chrome, letting users save and reuse AI prompts across websites. The feature builds on Gemini’s browser integration.

title: How to Use Google Chrome’s New AI-Powered ‘Skills’
description: The premade Skills available through the Gemini sidebar in Chrome include ways to maximize protein in recipes or summarize YouTube videos.

description:

title: Anthropic Opposes the Extreme AI Liability Bill That OpenAI Backed
description: Anthropic and OpenAI are clashing over a proposed Illinois law that would let AI labs largely off the hook for mass deaths and financial disasters.

title: Silicon Valley Is Spending Millions to Stop One of Its Own
description: Alex Bores, a former Palantir employee, helped pass one of the country’s toughest AI laws. Now Silicon Valley’s biggest names are trying to stop his rise to Congress.

title: You Can Soon Buy a $4,370 Humanoid Robot on AliExpress
description: Unitree is bringing its R1 to international markets. It arrives with some aerobatic capabilities and an entry-level price, but the question of what you'd actually do with it remains open.

title: Meta Is Warned That Facial Recognition Glasses Will Arm Sexual Predators
description: More than 70 organizations, including the ACLU, EPIC, and Fight for the Future, say the AI smart glasses feature would endanger abuse victims, immigrants, and LGBTQ+ people.

title: Distill Hiatus
description: After five years, Distill will be taking a break.

description: Reprogramming Neural CA to exhibit novel behaviour, using adversarial attacks.

title: Weight Banding
description: Weights in the final layer of common visual models appear as horizontal bands. We investigate how and why.
